text: The sagittal section shows a low rectal growth with a partial response, while the axial section reveals persistent lateral lymph nodes. The robotic setup includes a fenestrated bipolar in the R-I arm, a camera in the R-II arm, a monopolar scissor in the R-III arm, and a prograsp in the R-IV arm. The surgeon medializes the ureter and defines the urethrohypogastric fascia, ensuring the hypogastric nerve's safety. Dissection follows the obliterated umbilical ligament to define the vesico-hypogastric fascia and paravesical space, revealing the obturator nerve. The surgeon dissects nodal tissue over the external iliac vessels, moving from lateral to medial, and clears tissue over the femoral canal. The dissection continues proximally towards the iliac vessel bifurcation, clearing nodal tissue and identifying the proximal obturator nerve.

The surgeon identifies and dissects the proximal part of the obturator nerve, clearing nodal tissue around it. The obturator vessels are dissected, clipped, and divided using monopolar scissors. The tissue over the obturator nerve is dissected free, and nodal tissue is cleared over the internal iliac artery and obturator nerve. The dissection continues towards the pelvic floor, targeting lymph nodes draining from the low and mid rectum.

The surgeon performs dissection along the branches of the internal iliac artery, clearing tissue over the obliterated umbilical ligament. The dissection continues along the inferior vesicle artery, clearing nodal tissue over the artery and proceeding down towards the pelvic floor. The surgeon then dissects laterally over the pudendal vessels, clipping and dividing them.

The surgeon clears all nodal tissue over the pelvic floor, completing the pelvic lymph node dissection. The nodal tissue is reflected, and the specimen is delivered in total. The final field of dissection is inspected, showing intact urethrohypogastric fascia and preserved hypogastric nerve on the medial aspect. Dissection along the branches of the internal iliac vessels is noted, with the internal iliac vein crossing anterior to the artery branches, a variation identified during careful dissection. All nodal tissue along the inferior vesicle artery and pelvic floor is cleared, with critical structures identified and preserved.
